ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater (MERCURY / Mango Sandstorm) commodity-RMM first install via spear-phishing — ScreenConnect / Atera / SimpleHelp / RemoteUtilities
ucs.description: Detects the actor's signature initial-access chain: phishing email → PDF/HTML lure → archive on a file-sharing host (OneHub, Sync, TeraBox) → MSI/EXE that installs a legitimate RMM agent. Hunts the FIRST-EVER appearance of ScreenConnect/Atera/SimpleHelp/RemoteUtilities/Syncro on a device when the parent is a browser, mail client, MSI handler or Explorer (i.e. user-launched from a download), with the host absent from the 30-day RMM baseline.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientSetup.exe","AteraAgent.exe","SimpleHelpCustomer.exe","Remote Support.exe","rutserv.exe","rutview.exe","Syncro.Service.exe","AnyDesk.exe") AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","outlook.exe","msiexec.exe","explorer.exe","7zG.exe","WinRAR.exe") AND Processes.user!="*$" by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval firstTime=strftime(firstTime,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | join type=left host process_name [| tstats summariesonly=true earliest(_time) as baseline_first from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","AteraAgent.exe","SimpleHelpCustomer.exe","rutserv.exe","Syncro.Service.exe","AnyDesk.exe") earliest=-30d@d latest=-4h@h by host Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | where isnull(baseline_first)
ucs.kql:
let RmmBins = dynamic(["screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.clientsetup.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","ateraagent.exe","simplehelpcustomer.exe","remote support.exe","rutserv.exe","rutview.exe","syncro.service.exe","syncro.installer.exe","anydesk.exe"]);
let UserLaunchers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","outlook.exe","msiexec.exe","explorer.exe","7zg.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe"]);
let RmmBaseline = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(30d) .. ago(4h))
    | where FileName in~ (RmmBins)
    | summarize by DeviceId, FileName;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(4h)
| where FileName in~ (RmmBins)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"        // exclude machine accounts
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (UserLaunchers)
   or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\")
| join kind=leftanti RmmBaseline on DeviceId, FileName     // first-install only
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let RmmBins = dynamic(["ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientSetup.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","AteraAgent.exe","SimpleHelpCustomer.exe","rutserv.exe","rutview.exe","Syncro.Service.exe","AnyDesk.exe"]);
let UserLaunchers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","outlook.exe","msiexec.exe","explorer.exe","7zG.exe","WinRAR.exe"]);
let RmmBaseline = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(30d) .. ago(4h))
    | where EventID == 4688
    | extend BinName = tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1])
    | where BinName in~ (RmmBins)
    | summarize by Computer, BinName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(4h)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend BinName = tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1])
| extend ParentBin = tostring(split(ParentProcessName, @"\")[-1])
| where BinName in~ (RmmBins)
| where ParentBin in~ (UserLaunchers)
| join kind=leftanti RmmBaseline on Computer, BinName
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, BinName, NewProcessName,
          CommandLine, ParentBin, ParentProcessName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: MuddyWater commodity-RMM first install via spear-phishing
id: 7b3a6f24-1c47-4f9c-8a91-9c9e6f4b1d4a
status: experimental
description: Detects ScreenConnect/Atera/SimpleHelp/RemoteUtilities/Syncro installer or agent processes spawned by a browser, mail client, MSI handler or archive utility — the characteristic MuddyWater (MERCURY / Mango Sandstorm) phishing-to-RMM chain. Pair with a per-host baseline anti-join in your SIEM to suppress IT-led RMM rollouts.
author: Detection Engineering
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2022/04/12/tarrask-malware-uses-scheduled-tasks-for-defense-evasion/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2023/04/07/mercury-and-deev-pirate-leverage-destructive-attacks/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0069/
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe'
      - '\ScreenConnect.ClientSetup.exe'
      - '\ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe'
      - '\AteraAgent.exe'
      - '\SimpleHelpCustomer.exe'
      - '\Remote Support.exe'
      - '\rutserv.exe'
      - '\rutview.exe'
      - '\Syncro.Service.exe'
      - '\AnyDesk.exe'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\brave.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\7zG.exe'
      - '\WinRAR.exe'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_child and selection_parent and not filter_machine_acct
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - ParentCommandLine
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate IT-led RMM rollouts (allowlist by host/role)
  - Helpdesk-initiated AnyDesk/SimpleHelp sessions invited by the user
level: high
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1204.001
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1219
ucs.rationale: MuddyWater's defining 2022-2024 tradecraft (Microsoft MSTIC, Mandiant, Group-IB, CISA AA22-055A) is the abuse of legitimate RMM software for hands-on access — they specifically use ScreenConnect, Atera, SimpleHelp, RemoteUtilities and Syncro delivered via PDF lures that link to file-sharing services. Most APTs use bespoke implants; MERCURY's reliance on signed commodity RMM (T1219) means signature

ucs.title: [LLM] MuddyWater PowGoop loader — goopdate.dll side-loaded by GoogleUpdate.exe and PowerShell descent
ucs.description: Detects the PowGoop loader chain unique to MuddyWater: a legitimately-signed GoogleUpdate.exe is dropped to a non-Program-Files path and side-loads a malicious goopdate.dll which then spawns PowerShell to decrypt the next stage. Catches both the abnormal DLL load location and the GoogleUpdate→PowerShell child relationship.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_path) as parent_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="GoogleUpdate.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") AND Processes.user!="*$" by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_path Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | search NOT parent_path IN ("*\\Program Files\\Google\\*","*\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\*") | eval firstTime=strftime(firstTime,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
ucs.kql:
// Signal A — goopdate.dll loaded by GoogleUpdate.exe from a non-Google path
let SideLoad = DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName =~ "goopdate.dll"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "googleupdate.exe"
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\")
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files\Google\")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, FolderPath, SHA256,
              LoaderPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              LoaderSHA = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
              Signal = "goopdate.dll side-load from non-standard path";
// Signal B — GoogleUpdate.exe spawns PowerShell / cmd / rundll32
let ChildSpawn = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "googleupdate.exe"
    | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              FolderPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              SHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
              LoaderPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              LoaderSHA = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
              ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              Signal = strcat("GoogleUpdate spawned ", FileName);
union SideLoad, ChildSpawn
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// PowGoop signal via 4688 — GoogleUpdate.exe spawning a script/loader child from outside Program Files
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| extend ChildBin  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, @"\")[-1]))
| where ParentBin == "googleupdate.exe"
| where ChildBin in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe")
| where not(ParentProcessName startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\")
   and not(ParentProcessName startswith @"C:\Program Files\Google\")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: MuddyWater PowGoop side-load — GoogleUpdate.exe spawns scripting host
id: c4e7d12b-8b9a-4d2c-9d11-2f8a7c1d3e92
status: experimental
description: Detects the PowGoop loader behaviour used by MuddyWater (MERCURY / Mango Sandstorm / Static Kitten) where a relocated, legitimately-signed GoogleUpdate.exe side-loads a malicious goopdate.dll and spawns a scripting host (PowerShell / cmd / rundll32 / mshta) to decrypt the next stage. Filters out GoogleUpdate executions from the legitimate Program Files install paths.
references:
  - https://www.deepinstinct.com/blog/powgoop-malware-iranian-actor-muddywater
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0069/
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\GoogleUpdate.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
  filter_legit_path:
    ParentImage|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\Google\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_legit_path and not filter_machine_acct
fields:
  - ParentImage
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Portable / dev installs of Chrome auto-updater run from a custom location (rare in enterprise)
level: high
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
ucs.rationale: PowGoop is a documented MuddyWater-exclusive loader (Deep Instinct 2022, PaloAlto Unit 42, Microsoft MSTIC tracked under MERCURY / Mango Sandstorm). The author repurposes a legitimate signed GoogleUpdate.exe binary as a host for DLL side-loading via a malicious goopdate.dll. No legitimate Chrome / Google Update workflow drops GoogleUpdate.exe outside Program Files, and no benign workflow has it sp
